docs.title: Community Care at ESIIL
docs.text:
Welcome to the community care team repository, part of the Environmental Data Science Innovation and Impact Lab (ESIIL). This repository serves as the central hub for our working group community care documents at ESIIL as well as resources for our different working group cohorts.

This repository and GitHub Page is especially important to ESIIL Working Group Collaboration Leads (CLs). CLs should check back on this repository semi-frequently to find any important updates, such as new resources and materials to assist with building out the working group collaboration plan and other teaming agreements.

docs.title: A Resource for CLs
docs.text:
This repository and GitHub Page is especially made to help ESIIL Working Group CLs navigate their role and responsibilities. As such, while the ESIIL Community Care GitHub Page provides a variety of helpful resources we want to caution individual team members from making duplicate templates and copies that their CL has already made.

If you are a current ESIIL Working Group, please contact your CL first to ensure that you are not making duplicate copies of items.

docs.title: Collaborators
docs.text:
• Susan Sullivan, Community Care Lead
  • Kayleigh Ward, Team Science Postdoctoral Associate

docs.title: Contributing to our Community Care and Working Group Resources
docs.text: At this time we only welcome contributions from ESIIL affiliated staff and working groups. Please contact Kayleigh Ward directly. If you are just exploring community care at ESIIL we also recommend checking out our all-star working groups.

docs.title: Tracking Team AI Use
docs.text:
In our ESIIL Collaboration Lead Guidelines and Resources we provide a link to our ESIIL AI/ML Scoreboard Tracker.

This AI/ML scoreboard helps teams streamline their AI tracking process for publications, products, and team agreements. This template can be adjusted to each group's needs. Groups can list their members by product (see sheet tabs), include the AI model used, and which section of the product or manuscript AI was used for.

In the template, empty cells under the different AI use categories assumes no AI was used for that purpose. The types of AI use categories are based off of current top journal requirements. There is also an AI checkpoint sheet where teams can list their AI team agreements to cross reference with their products. Overall this scoreboard will help your team work better together and encourage transparency.

docs.title: Our Code of Conduct
docs.text:
All working groups at ESIIL are beholden to our Code of Conduct. All team members, PIs, and the collaboration lead is responsible for having read our Code of Conduct prior to arriving at ESIIL. If you have questions about our Code of Conduct please reach out to our Community Care Lead, Susan Sullivan. You can find a link to our Code below.

Batchelor, R., SULLIVAN, S., Iglesias, V., John Parker, Stavros, E. N., Anne Gold, & BALCH, J. (2026). ESIIL Code of Conduct and Community Collaboration Guidelines.

docs.title: ESIIL Guidelines for Intellectual Contributions and Credit
Giving Credit Where It's Due
docs.text:
At ESIIL we know navigating authorship agreements and recognizing team contributions is not a simple process. To help faciliate and provide guidance on crediting contributions, all ESIIL working groups are responsible for reading our Guidelines for Intellectual Contributions and Credit. Please find these guidelines below.

BALCH, J., Nagy, R. C., Mary Rose Posa, SULLIVAN, S., Batchelor, R., & Tuff, T. (2025). ESIIL Guidelines for Intellectual Contributions and Credit

docs.title: Tracking Team Contributions
docs.text:
Within the ESIIL Guidelines for Intellectual Contributions and Credit you will find a link to our CRediT Contributions Table. To make that easier to access we have also provided a direct copy template of that table here.

You will also find a link to our Authorship Agreement Form in this same Guidelines document. The authorship agreement form is different from the tracker which helps team members log or check off what they contributed to any team product, but especially manuscripts. The Authorship Agreement Form is the criteria that the group comes to a shared agreement on in terms of significant, substantial and modest contributions.

As a result, the CRediT Table and this Authorship Agreement Form work together to create the contribution and credit backbone of the working group.

docs.title: Representative Role and Responsibilities
docs.text:
The Collaboration Lead is a new ESIIL Working Group role, meant to be similar to that of the technical lead. This role has been established to ensure groups are well supported in their work together, just as they are in their technical advances.

The person in this role does not need to have prior experience in this role, only an interest in learning more about collaboration skills and a willingness to be the point person to ensure ESIIL collaboration tools are being used systematically.

The Collaboration Lead will:

  • Attend a 2.5 hr interactive workshop with other collaboration leads and with ESIIL team science and community care staff. This workshop will allow leads to get a more in-depth understanding of ESIIL tools and support avenues.
  • Briefly communicate with the ESIIL working group support staff regularly
  • Lead team agreement discussions, especially during the first in-person meeting and regularly throughout the period of work.
  • Lead regular review of the intellectual credit matrix and contributions table, especially as team member responsibilities change or products are developed.
  • Be aware of group collaboration resources from ESIIL and be able to point team members to ESIIL collaboration supports as needed.

The Collaboration Lead may change over the course of the working group. If that happens, ESIIL staff will work with the new Lead to be sure they are prepared for the role.

docs.title: Masterlist of Resources for CLs
docs.text:
Most of the resources for your team are available from this repository and its GitHub Page. In the tabs before this section, we have provided a link to the ESIIL Code of Conduct and our Guides for Intellectual Contributions and Credit. These are also linked it your own team's personal ESIIL OASIS repository.

You can find the viewable masterlist of CL resources here. This document will be regularly updated. For items that are tools or templates for your team we have added individual tabs for them, such as the CRedIT Contributions Table, AI Scoreboard and other training and materials.

docs.title: Navigating Collaboration Plans and Agreements
docs.text:
To help ESIIL Working Groups be successful, effective and enjoyable we recommend teams formulate their group agreements early on in the collaboration process. To assist with that, the team science and community care teams have created a variety of templates, examples, slidedecks, and questions to help your team navigate this process.

In our ESIIL Collaboration Lead Guidelines and Resources you can find the following resources:

  • ESIIL Working Group Norms and Collaboration Plan Firestarter Questions
  • ESIIL Working Group Norms and Collaboration Plan Fillable
  • ESIIL Working Group Norms and Collaboration Plan Example

With our ESIIL Collaboration Lead Guidelines and Resources: Team Agreements Template Slidedeck we provide language and example questions for working groups to proactively discuss key components of their collaboration and to work through important team agreements. Here we have included agreements related to interpersonal dynamics, logistics, technical aspects as well as data sharing and attribution.

ESIIL CLs have direct access to this slidedeck from the masterlist document. We recommend making a copy of this slidedeck from that resource.
